title: ActiveCampaign vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign and Substack for email marketing. Find the best tool for your business needs, pricing, and features.
intro: ActiveCampaign and Substack serve vastly different email marketing needs. ActiveCampaign is a comprehensive business automation and CRM platform, while Substack is a creator-focused newsletter platform designed for monetization. Understanding their distinct purposes will help you choose the right tool for your specific use case.

comparison_table.tool_a: Advanced automation, lead scoring, SMS, dynamic content
comparison_table.tool_b: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in audience

choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and CRM capabilities for your business
You want to track leads, score prospects, and manage complex customer journeys
You require SMS messaging, site tracking, and dynamic content personalization
choose_b_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize your audience directly
You prefer a free platform with revenue-sharing over upfront costs
You want to combine newsletter publishing with podcast hosting and audience recommendations

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign is better for businesses requiring sophisticated automation, CRM, and lead management. Substack is superior for creators and writers focused on direct monetization through paid subscriptions. They're designed for different audiences rather than competing directly.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is free to start with a revenue-sharing model (Substack takes a cut of paid subscriptions). ActiveCampaign requires a minimum $15/month investment. However, Substack's costs scale with revenue, while ActiveCampaign's pricing is predictable and may be cheaper at scale for businesses with large subscriber bases.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Substack and import it into ActiveCampaign. However, the platforms serve different purposes—ActiveCampaign won't replicate Substack's paid subscription and monetization features. Consider your long-term goals before switching between them.
